_comment: Update these numbers periodically. Sources listed below.
sources: Daily Beast (Jun 2, 2026): 861 posts in May 2026 alone, avg 27/day — new daily rate record
NPR (May 8, 2026): 2,249 posts in first 4 months of 2026, avg 19/day
WCVB/Get the Facts: 6,168 posts in full year 2025, avg 18/day
Washington Post (Jun 2025): 2,262 posts in first 132 days of 2nd term
Roll Call (Oct 2024): 24+ posts/day avg through Oct 2024

yearData.note: 2,249 thru Apr (NPR) + 861 in May (Daily Beast) = 3,110. May avg 27/day, new record pace.

records.label: Most posts in one month (so far)
records.note: May 2026 — Daily Beast analysis published Jun 2. Avg 27/day. June isn't over yet either.

records.label: Most posts in one evening
records.note: Dec 1, 2025 — a late-night spree covering Christmas, Michelle Obama, Home Alone 2, and policy attacks.

records.label: Posts in 3 hours
records.note: May 11–12, 2026 — 55 posts between 10:14 PM and 1 AM. Snopes fact-checked the count.

records.label: Posts in 36 minutes
records.note: Jan 29, 2026 — 9:23 to 9:59 PM Eastern. Described as 'wild conspiracy theories and ego-boosting posts.'

records.label: Posts during VP debate
records.note: Oct 1, 2024 — posted play-by-play for 90 minutes straight during Vance vs. Walz.
